zh.Polesworth, North Warwickshire Borough,: 英國英格蘭瓦維其可爾波萊斯沃斯
zh.Llansadwrn, Wales: 英國威爾斯卡馬特史爾利安薩德萊
zh.lifeSummary: Sir Rhys ap Gruffudd於1275年出生在英國威爾斯卡馬特史爾利安薩德萊。他和Isabel Ferch Richard至少有1個兒子。他去世於1356年5月10日在英國威爾斯卡馬特史爾卡馬特享年81歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭瓦維其可爾波萊斯沃斯。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Sir Rhys ap Gruffudd于1275年出生在英国威尔斯卡馬特史爾利安薩德萊。他和Isabel Ferch Richard至少有1个儿子。他去世于1356年5月10日在英国威尔斯卡馬特史爾卡馬特享年81岁，埋葬在英国英格兰瓦維其可爾波萊斯沃斯。

nameMeanings.name: ap
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Hungarian Geza, Jeno, Arpad, Attila, Csaba.

Hungarian: variant of   Papp  , meaning ‘priest (of the Catholic or Orthodox Church)’. This surname is also found in Romania, Slovakia, Croatia, and Serbia, and also among Rusyns (in Serbia and Croatia). See also   Pop   2 and   Popa  .

variants.name: Papp
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Hungarian Laszlo, Zoltan, Geza, Istvan, Gabor, Miklos, Tibor, Arpad, Denes, Imre, Attila.
German (Bavaria, Tyrol): from Middle High German pap ‘baby food’, Dutch pap ‘porridge, pap’, hence perhaps a nickname denoting someone who had lost his teeth or subsisted on a meager diet.

Hungarian: from pap ‘priest (of the Catholic or Orthodox Church)’, probably an occupational name for someone in the service of a priest or a nickname for a pious person. This surname is also found in Romania and Slovakia, and also among Rusyns. Compare Pap and Pop .

nameMeanings.name: Gruffudd
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Griffith
meanings: Welsh (Carmarthenshire and western England): from the Old Welsh personal name Gruffudd, Old Welsh Grip(p)iud, composed of the elements grip, of uncertain significance, + iud ‘chief, lord’.

nameMeanings.name: Sir
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

variants.name: George
meanings: Via Old French and Latin, from Greek Georgios (a derivative of geōrgos ‘farmer’, from gē ‘earth’ + ergein ‘to work’). This was the name of several early saints, including the shadowy figure who is now the patron of England (as well as of Germany and Portugal). If the saint existed at all, he was perhaps martyred in Palestine in the persecutions of Christians instigated by the Emperor Diocletian at the beginning of the 4th century. The popular legend in which the hero slays a dragon is a medieval Italian invention. He was for a long time a more important saint in the Orthodox Church than in the West, and the name was not much used in England during the Middle Ages, even after St George came to be regarded as the patron of England in the 14th century. Its use increased from the 1400s, and by 1500 it was regularly among the most popular male names. This popularity was reinforced when George I came to the throne in 1714 , bringing this name with him from Germany. It has been one of the most popular English boys' names ever since.

nameMeanings.name: Rhys
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Traditional name meaning ‘ardour’; borne in the Middle Ages by various rulers in south-west Wales, including  Rhys ap Tewdur  (  d. 1093 ) and  Rhys ap Gruffudd  (  1132–97 ).

variants.name: Reece
meanings: Anglicized spelling of Rhys . The name is now in widespread and frequent use outside Wales, in many cases representing a transferred use of the surname so spelled, which is derived from the given name.

variants.name: Rees
meanings: Anglicized spelling of Welsh Rhys, in some cases representing a transferred use of the surname so spelled, which is derived from the Welsh given name.

en.lifeSummary: Sir Rhys ap Gruffudd was born in 1275, in Llansadwrn, Carmarthenshire, Wales, United Kingdom. He had at least 1 son with Isabel Ferch Richard. He died on 10 May 1356, in Carmarthen, Carmarthenshire, Wales, United Kingdom, at the age of 81, and was buried in Polesworth, Warwickshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Sir Rhys ap Gruffudd est né en 1275, à Llansadwrn, Carmarthenshire, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni. Il a eu au moins 1 fils avec Isabel Ferch Richard. Il est décédé le 10 mai 1356, à Carmarthen, Carmarthenshire, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 81 ans, et a été enterré à Polesworth, Warwickshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

Mai 1356 im Alter von 81 Jahren in Carmarthen, Carmarthenshire, Wales, United Kingdom und wurde in Polesworth, Warwickshire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Sir Rhys ap Gruffudd è nato il nel 1275 nel Llansadwrn, Carmarthenshire, Galles, Regno Unito. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio con Isabel Ferch Richard. Morì il 10 maggio 1356, nel Carmarthen, Carmarthenshire, Galles, Regno Unito, all'età di 81 anni, e fu sepolto a Polesworth, Warwickshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.Llansadwrn, Wales: イギリスウェールズカーマースエンシュアイアランサドウルン
ja.lifeSummary: Sir Rhys ap Gruffuddは1275年にイギリスウェールズカーマースエンシュアイアランサドウルンで生まれた。彼はIsabel Ferch Richardとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼は1356年5月10日にイギリスウェールズカーマースエンシュアイアカーマースンで81歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスワーウィクシュアイアポレスウオルスにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Rhys ap Gruffudd(은)는 1275년 영국 웨일스 카마테실르 랜사드른에서 태어났다. 그는 Isabel Ferch Richard(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1356년 5월 10일 영국 웨일스 카마테실르 카마테에서 81세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 워윅실르 포레스워스에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Sir Rhys ap Gruffudd родился в 1275 г. в местности ллансадрн, кармартеншир, Уэльс, Великобритания. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын от Isabel Ferch Richard. Он умер 10 мая 1356 г. в местности кармартен, кармартеншир, Уэльс, Великобритания в возрасте 81 год и был похоронен в местности полесворт, варвиккшир, Англия, Великобритания.
